input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest stable)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8  # or .9 depending on your target
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to find exact name/version
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target needs WinUI/UWP SDK)

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or modify existing install via command line:
# Required workload IDs for MAUI:
#   Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat (MAUI)
#   Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs (WinUI)

& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --passive
```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

```powershell
# MAUI installs Android SDK automatically, but to manage it manually:

# Find where MAUI put the Android SDK
dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n 2>&1 | Select-String "AndroidSdkPath"

# Or check the default location:
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    $androidSdk = "$env:PROGRAMFILES\Android\android-sdk"
}

# List installed SDK packages
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Accept licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Install specific API levels dynamically (find latest stable)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>&1 |
    Select-String "platforms;android-\d+" |
    Sort-Object -Descending | Select-Object -First 5

# Install what you need (example)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "platforms;android-34" `
    "build-tools;34.0.0" `
    "platform-tools"
```

## 5. Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
# Install emulator + system image
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "emulator" `
    "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" `
    --device "pixel_6"
```

## 6. Enable Windows Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
# Run as Administrator
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d 1
```

## 7. Verify Everything

```powershell
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest -o ./MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run diagnostics if anything fails
dotnet workload repair
```

## 8. Discover Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# Latest .NET SDK available
winget show Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --versions

# Installed Android build-tools
Get-ChildItem "$androidSdk\build-tools" | Select-Object Name

# Target framework monikers your SDK supports
dotnet --list-runtimes

# Check MAUI workload version
dotnet workload list --machine-readable | ConvertFrom-Json
```

---

**Key gotcha:** The Windows target requires the Windows App SDK which is bundled with the VS workload — you can't easily get it standalone. If you only use VS Code, install the [Build Tools for Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/downloads/#build-tools-for-visual-studio-2022) with the same component IDs above.
